name: Exa Code MCP Server
what_it_does: Official MCP server from Exa Labs (exa-labs org) for code-specific search. Enables AI agents to find open-source libraries and fetch contextual code snippets by version — accelerating development workflows by providing up-to-date library documentation and usage examples. Specialized code search built on Exa's neural search infrastructure.
use_cases: Finding open-source library versions and code examples from coding assistant agents
Fetching documentation and code snippets for specific library versions from development agents
Discovering relevant libraries for specific programming tasks from architecture agents
Getting up-to-date usage examples for rapidly evolving libraries from AI coding tools
not_for: General web search (Exa has a general search MCP for that)
Private/internal code search (searches public open-source libraries)
best_when: A coding assistant agent needs to find current library documentation, code examples, or open-source packages for specific programming tasks — ensuring agents use current API signatures rather than stale training data.
avoid_when: You need general web search beyond code and libraries. The general Exa MCP covers broader search use cases.

auth.notes: Exa API key required. Register at exa.ai for free tier access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier with limited queries; check exa.ai for current limits
pricing.paid_tiers: Usage-based pricing for higher query volume
pricing.notes: Exa offers a generous free tier. MCP server is free open source from official exa-labs org.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limited by Exa API tier; free tier has per-month limits

known_agent_gotchas: Code search results are from Exa's crawl — may not include very new or private repositories
Version-specific snippet fetching depends on Exa's indexing frequency — verify for cutting-edge library releases
Official from exa-labs org — Exa is a well-funded search startup with active development
Complement with other search for non-code queries; this MCP is code-search optimized
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS to Exa API. API key in environment variable. Searches public code — no sensitive data transmitted. Official Exa implementation.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS. API key auth. Public code search — minimal sensitive data risk. Official implementation from Exa Labs.
